code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of building‑related triples into a single fluent sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping from predicate to a readable phrase; placeholders {subj} and {obj} will be replaced.
    # Templates that produce a fragment *without* repeating the subject.
    # The subject will be added once at the beginning of the sentence.
    fragment_templates = {
        "architecturalStyle": "has an architectural style of {obj}",
        "buildingStartDate": "started construction in {obj}",
        "completionDate": "was completed in {obj}",
        "floorCount": "has {obj} floors",
        "location": "located in {obj}",
        "cost": "costs {obj}",
        "floorArea": "has a floor area of {obj}",
        "architect": "designed by {obj}",
        "owner": "is owned by {obj}",
        "formerName": "was formerly known as {obj}",
        "height": "has a height of {obj} metres",
        "buildingType": "is a {obj}",
        "developer": "was developed by {obj}",
        "tenant": "has tenant {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {obj}",
        "country": "is in {obj}",
        "currentTenants": "currently houses {obj}",
        "address": "has address {obj}",
        "inaugurationDate": "was inaugurated on {obj}",
        "birthPlace": "was born in {obj}",
        "deathPlace": "died in {obj}",
        "bedCount": "has {obj} beds",
        "region": "is in the region {obj}",
        "state": "is in the state {obj}",
        "website": "website is {obj}",
        "yearOfConstruction": "was constructed in {obj}",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "has NRHP reference number {obj}",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "was added to the NRHP on {obj}",
        "significantBuilding": "is known for the building {obj}",
        "governingBody": "is governed by {obj}",
        "leader": "the leader of {subj} is {obj}",
        "chancellor": "the chancellor of {subj} is {obj}",
        "governmentType": "has government type {obj}",
        "capital": "the capital of {subj} is {obj}",
        "language": "speaks {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {obj}",
        "currency": "uses currency {obj}",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group {obj}",
        "origin": "originates from {obj}",
        "significantProject": "has significant project {obj}",
        "foundationPlace": "was founded in {obj}",
        "demonym": "people are called {obj}",
        "numberOfRooms": "has {obj} rooms",
        "keyPerson": "has key person {obj}",
        "architecture": "has architecture {obj}",
        "postalCode": "has postal code {obj}",
    }

    # Build fragments for each triple
    fragments = []
    for triple in triples:
        tmpl = fragment_templates.get(triple.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            # Some templates need the subject inside (e.g., leader, capital)
            if "{subj}" in tmpl:
                fragment = tmpl.format(subj=triple.subject, obj=triple.object)
            else:
                fragment = tmpl.format(obj=triple.object)
        else:
            # Fallback generic rendering
            fragment = f"{triple.predicate} {triple.object}"
        fragments.append(fragment)

    # Assemble the final sentence
    subject = triples[0].subject
    if not fragments:
        return subject + "."
    if len(fragments) == 1:
        sentence = f"{subject} {fragments[0]}."
    else:
        # Join all but the last with commas
        middle = ", ".join(fragments[:-1])
        # Add Oxford comma before the last fragment
        sentence = f"{subject}, {middle}, and {fragments[-1]}."
    # Ensure first character is capitalized (subject already capitalized)
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
